stories.title: Iran warns U.S. against ground invasion, as Pakistan holds diplomatic talks
stories.description: A high-ranking Iranian official has accused the U.S. of planning a ground invasion as part of the next stage in the Iran war, and said such an intervention would be met with force.

stories.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels enter the fighting in the Middle East
stories.description: At least 15 U.S. military personnel were wounded in an Iranian attack on Saudi Arabia and Yemen's Houthi rebels entered the war with an attack on Israel.

stories.title: Over a dozen U.S. soldiers injured in attack on Saudi base as Iran-backed Houthis enter war
stories.description: An Iranian strike on an air base in Saudi Arabia wounded at least 15 U.S. service members. Israel also said it intercepted a missile launched from Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen.

stories.title: Senate votes to fund most of DHS. And, Trump extends Iran's deadline to reopen strait
stories.description: The Senate has voted to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security. And, President Trump extends the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Iranian missile hits an airbase in Saudi Arabia, injuring U.S. troops
stories.description: A U.S. official said several U.S. service members were wounded in the strike and some aircraft were apparently damaged as well. Iran has targeted U.S. bases throughout the region since the war began.

stories.title: Iran-backed Houthis join war with attack against Israel
stories.description: There are fears the Iranian proxy may attack shipping in the Red Sea, adding to global economic woes.

stories.title: Potential Houthi threat to Red Sea shipping could further damage global economy
stories.description: The Iran-backed group could bring a second crucial waterway to a standstill, writes Sebastian Usher.

stories.title: Three Lebanese journalists killed in Israeli strike, say broadcasters
stories.description: The Israeli military confirms it killed Ali Shoeib from the Hezbollah-affiliated Al Manar TV.

stories.title: 'My daughter is under the rubble': Inside Tehran as civilian toll of strikes rises
stories.description: One month since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran, Tehran residents tell the BBC their lives have been devastated.

stories.title: Gaza peace doubts deepen as attention shifts to Iran
stories.description: With the world focused on the Iran war, there is increasing uncertainty about what happens next for Gaza.

stories.title: Palestinian man killed as death toll from West Bank settler violence climbs
stories.description: Mohammad al-Malhi is the seventh Palestinian killed by a settler in a surge of violence since the start of the war with Iran.

stories.title: UN human rights chief calls on US to conclude probe into Iran school strike
stories.description: The strike - which killed at least 168 people, mostly children - "evoked a visceral horror", Volker Türk said.

stories.title: Ukraine signs deal with Saudi Arabia offering drone expertise
stories.description: Volodymyr Zelensky says the Gulf kingdom faces the same type of attacks from Iran that Kyiv has been resisting.

stories.title: Israel says it has killed Iran's navy chief overseeing Strait of Hormuz blockade
stories.description: Israel's defence minister said a number of other "senior Navy command officials" have also been killed. Iran has not yet commented.

stories.title: Trump's Iran strategy is to pursue two off-ramps at once
stories.description: The White House insists the president is dictating events, but even some Republicans are frustrated by mixed messaging about his war aims.

stories.title: Video shows US missile likely used in deadly strike on Iranian town, experts say
stories.description: The analysts identified a Precision Strike Missile (PrSM) in footage of the strike on the southern town of Lamerd.

stories.title: Is Trump's pause on attacking Iranian energy for diplomacy or an escalation?
stories.description: The US president's commitment to deadlines is fluid but he uses them for a purpose, writes the BBC's James Landale.

stories.title: How the Hormuz closure could affect food, medicines and smartphones
stories.description: The price of a wide range of goods - from food, to smartphones, to medicines - could be affected by the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: A committed pharmacist and a homesick blogger – the Iranian civilians killed in the war
stories.description: The toll on civilians is mounting fast as the US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

stories.title: Zelensky visits Jordan on Middle East tour to bolster security ties
stories.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said he was in Jordan on Sunday as part of a regional tour focused on security cooperation, after Kyiv deployed anti-drone experts and struck defence agreements with several Gulf states amid rising tensions linked to Iran.

stories.title: From the Iran war to the US petrol pumps
stories.description: 30 days into the war in Iran, the key Strait of Hormuz is still partially closed, making prices of oil rise, considerably affecting businesses and citizens all around the world. Business owner John Catsimatidis decrypts this situation with France 24's Gavin Lee.

stories.title: Massive protests against Trump across US
stories.description: Huge crowds protested Saturday against US President Donald Trump's actions and the war in Iran in "No Kings" rallies across the US and in Europe. Minnesota took centre stage, with thousands of people standing shoulder-to-shoulder to celebrate resistance to Trump's aggressive immi...

stories.title: Day of zero waste: International Chef Massimo Bottura speaks to France 24
stories.description: Monday marks the UN’s International Day of Zero Waste, an occasion aimed at raising awareness about sustainability, with this year’s spotlight on food. Each year, roughly one billion tonnes of edible food - nearly one-fifth of what is available to consumers - ends up being discar...

stories.title: Qatari news channel AI Araby condemns attack on its office
stories.description: Iran’s parliamentary speaker has cautioned the United States against launching a ground invasion, warning that Tehran could intensify attacks on US allies, according to state media. Meanwhile, an American-born Israeli soldier has been killed during fighting in southern Lebanon, o...

stories.title: Iran: 3.500 US troops arrive in the region
stories.description: Iran will target the USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier "as soon as" it comes within range, the country's navy chief said Sunday. Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf earlier accused Washington of plotting a ground attack on Iran even as it claims to be engaging ...

stories.title: Mediation talks in Islamabad: Middle East powers meet to discuss how to end the fighting
stories.description: The United States is plotting a ground attack on Iran even as it claims to be engaging in diplomatic efforts, Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said Sunday, as more American marines arrive in the region. The Israeli army earlier announced the death in combat of ...

stories.title: Anti-war protests in Israel: Hundreds gather in Tel-Aviv
stories.description: Hundreds gathered in Tel Aviv on Saturday to protest the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran, the latest in a series of weekly demonstrations since the conflict began a month ago. Participation now appears to be growing, prompting a tough response from Israeli authorities, who moved to...

stories.title: Tehran threatens to target US universities in the region
stories.description: Overnight, powerful explosions were reported in both eastern and western parts of Tehran, lighting up the capital’s skyline. In the south of the country, state media said US-Israeli strikes killed five people in the city of Bandar Khamir, near the strategic Strait of Hormuz. Iran...

stories.title: 'No Kings' rallies draw crowds across US
stories.description: Huge crowds protested Saturday against US President Donald Trump's actions and the war in Iran in "No Kings" rallies across the US and in Europe. Minnesota took centre stage, with thousands of people standing shoulder-to-shoulder to celebrate resistance to Trump's aggressive immi...

stories.title: Three Lebanese journalists killed in strikes
stories.description: Israel said early Sunday that one of its soldiers had been killed during clashes with Iran-backed Hezbollah in southern Lebanon.The announcement follows an Israeli airstrike on Saturday that killed three journalists, including a veteran correspondent for Hezbollah’s television ne...

stories.title: Yemen's rebels could further threaten global shipping
stories.description: Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen have carried out a second missile strike against Israel and have pledged to continue their attacks in the coming days. These assaults have heightened fears that the conflict could expand into the Red Sea region, where Saudi Arabia has already be...

stories.title: Cuba is not alone, deputy FM says as US threats grow
stories.description: Cuba's deputy foreign minister says island will resist US pressure as sanctions deepen, blackouts and tensions escalate.

stories.title: Republican Mace says Congress must approve any US troop deployment to Iran
stories.description: Despite general support for war, several Republicans say boots on the ground would be a political Rubicon for Trump.

stories.title: ‘We can insure the ship, but we cannot insure a human life.’
stories.description: International Maritime Organization chief Arsenio Dominguez warns of 20,000 seafarers stranded in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Houthis open new front against Israel, is Red Sea shipping at risk?
stories.description: The Houthis in Yemen have launched their first attacks on Israel, opening a new front in the month-long regional war.

stories.title: Iran hits industrial zone in southern Israel
stories.description: An Iranian missile barrage has caused a fire a chemical plant near Beersheba in southern Israel.

stories.title: Israeli police block Catholic cardinal from Holy Sepulchre on Palm Sunday
stories.description: Since the US and Israel launched a war on Iran, Israel has closed holy sites in Jerusalem, citing safety concerns.

stories.title: Houthis open new front in Iran war: Will Yemeni group block Bab al-Mandeb?
stories.description: Blockade of strait, one of the world's busiest maritime routes, would prove disastrous for global economy.

stories.title: How the US memeifies its war on Iran
stories.description: Al Jazeera’s Linh Nguyen explains the new era of war propaganda.

stories.title: Pakistan maintains ‘delicate balancing act’ as it hosts Iran talks
stories.description: Foreign ministers meet in Islamabad, seeking to de-escalate the US-Israel war on Iran.

stories.title: ‘Nowhere is really safe’: Iranian dissidents grapple with US war in Iran
stories.description: Critics of Iran's government say they fear retribution while in exile, as well as growing anti-immigrant sentiment.

stories.title: Iran ‘hits’ US AWACS, air tankers: What else has it targeted in past month?
stories.description: Iran reportedly hits E-3 Sentry flying radars, which track drones, missiles, aircraft from hundreds of kilometres away.

stories.title: Pakistan hosts four-nation bid to encourage US, Iran towards diplomacy
stories.description: Top diplomats of Pakistan, Egypt, Saudi Arabia and Turkiye meet to prepare ground for de-escalation in war on Iran.

stories.title: ‘We will see a new government in Venezuela, in Cuba and in Iran’
stories.description: Senator Ted Cruz says US President Donald Trump’s approach could lead to government changes in Venezuela, Cuba and Iran.

stories.title: Dozens detained in Tel Aviv as anti-war protest turns violent
stories.description: Police in Tel Aviv dispersed protesters opposing the US-Israeli war on Iran on Saturday. Up to 18 people were arrested.

stories.title: Pentagon readies ‘for weeks of US ground operations’ in Iran
stories.description: Uncertainty looms over Trump's approval of the Pentagon's plans for limited ground operations in Iran's strategic regions.

stories.title: Trump cannot declare victory if Iran continues launching missiles at Israel — US expert
stories.description: Ritter also noted that declarations of victory would look questionable if the Strait of Hormuz remained blocked

stories.title: Iran-US talks may take place in Islamabad in coming days — Pakistani foreign minister
stories.description: Pakistan is very happy that both Iran and the US have expressed their confidence in Pakistan to facilitate the talks, Dar added

stories.title: US-Israeli strikes hit Iran’s Isfahan University of Technology
stories.description: According to the media outlet, some of the university’s buildings were damaged and at least four employees suffered injuries

stories.title: Israel carries out 140 strikes on targets in western, central Iran in past day — army
stories.description: According to the Israeli military, ballistic missile storage and launch sites, and air defense facilities were hit

stories.title: Top diplomats of Pakistan, Egypt, Saudi Arabia, Turkey meet in Islamabad
stories.description: According to a Pakistani Foreign Ministry source, the parties are discussing communication between Washington and Tehran

stories.title: Russia wishes the US good luck in settlement with Iran — Kremlin aide
stories.description: The continuation of bombing is just terrific, Russian presidential aide Yury Ushakov said

stories.title: Ammunition depots attacked at US military bases — Iran’s IRGC
stories.description: They are located in Iraq, Kuwait and Saudi Arabia, the IRGC press service said

stories.title: US attack against Iran caused harmful consequences for peace — Peskov
stories.description: These implication will not probably be of a short term, the Kremlin spokesman said

stories.title: Board of Peace appears less current amid US aggression in Iran — Peskov
stories.description: Russia has not yet decided whether to enter the Board of Peace or not, the Kremlin spokesman said

stories.title: Five people killed in bombing of Iranian port — TV
stories.description: Two ships caught fire during the attack, Al Hadath television reported

stories.title: Iran launches another missile strike on Israeli territory — IDF
stories.description: Defense systems are operating to intercept the threat

stories.title: US gasoline prices jump about $1 since Iran conflict escalation
stories.description: According to the American Automobile Association, Regular gasoline averaged $4.138 per gallon in Washington as of March 28

stories.title: Pentagon prepares for possible US ground operations in Iran — media
stories.description: The ground campaign could last several weeks
